sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׁכַּב, inf. שְׁכַב with suff. שִׁכְבָהּ Gen 19:33, and שָׁכְבְּךָ (from שְׁכֹב), Deu 6:7 imp. שְׁכַב, with ה parag. שִׁכְבִה to lie, to lie down. (Syr., Ch., and Eth. ሰከበ፡ id., but Arab. سكب to pour out, which is done by inclining a vessel. The primary stock is כב, כף, which has the power of bending, inclining oneself, compare under כָּפַף, Gr. Lat. κύπτω, cubo, cumbo. As to a sibilant prefixed to a biliteral stock to form triliterals, see p. 783, A. ) Psa 68:14, “would that ye lay amongst the stalls” (shepherds to take rest). Isa 50:11, “ye shall lie down in sorrow.” 1Ki 3:19, “(the woman) had lain upon it” (the child), and had smothered it; also to lay one’s self down (fich legen), Eze 4:6, “lie on thy right side,” lege dich auf die rechte Seite. Specially used-(a) of those who are going to sleep, to lay oneself down to sleep, Gen 19:4 28:11 1Sa 3:5, 1Sa 3:6, 1Sa 3:9 Psa 3:6 4:9 1Ki 19:5 Job 7:4 Pro 3:24 and as being asleep, 1Sa 3:2 2Sa 11:9 1Sa 26:7 1Ki 21:27 also as taking rest, Psa 68:14 hence to rest, Ecc 2:23, “even at night his heart doth not rest.” Job 30:17, “my gnawing (pains) take no rest.”-(b) used of those who are sick, 2Sa 13:6 2Ki 9:16.-(c) of mourners, 2Sa 12:16.-(d) of those who are dying (to lay oneself down), very often in speaking of the death of kings in the phrase, וַיִּשְׁכַּב עִם אֲבֹותָיו 1Ki 2:10 11:43 14:20 , 31 1Ki 14:31 15:8 , 24 1Ki 15:24 16:6 , 28 1Ki 16:28and of the dead, Isa 14:8, מֵאָז שָׁכַבְתָּ “since thou wast laid down,” hast died [wast destroyed]. Isa 14:18 43:17 Job 3:13 20:11 21:26 . שֹׁכְבֶי קֶבֶר those who lie in the grave, Psa 88:6.-(e) used of those who lie with a woman, followed by עִם Gen 26:10 30:15 , 16 Gen 30:16 34:2 39:7 , 12 Gen 39:12 Exo 22:15 Deu 22:23, seq.; 2Sa 12:11 and אֵת Gen 35:22 1Sa 2:22 with suff. אֹתִי, אֹתָהּ Gen 34:2 Lev 15:18, 24 Lev 15:24 (once used of a woman, Gen 19:32); also with an acc. Deu 28:30, in קרי (where in the text there is שָׁגַל). Hence-

Niphal, to be lain with, ravished (used of a woman), Isa 13:16 Zec 14:2 and-

Pual, id. Jer 3:2 קרי.

Hiphil-

(1) to make any one lie down, to prostrate, 2Sa 8:2 to lay down, 1Ki 17:19 to cause to rest, Hos 2:20.

(2) to pour out a vessel (i.q. Arab. سكب to pour out, see above as to this meaning), Job 38:37.

Hophal הָשְׁכַּב, part. מָשְׁכָּב to be prostrated, to lie, 2Ki 4:32 Eze 32:19, 32 Eze 32:32.

Derivatives, שְׁכָבָה, שְׁכֹבֶת, מִשְׁכָּב.
